definitions.text: To move or pass from one place, person or thing to another.
examples: to transfer the laws of one country to another; to transfer suspicion

definitions.text: To convey the impression of (something) from one surface to another.
examples: to transfer drawings or engravings to a lithographic stone

definitions.text: To exit one mass transit vehicle and board another (typically one belonging to a different line or mode of transportation) to continue a journey.
The act of conveying or removing something from one place, person or thing to another.
An instance of conveying or removing from one place, person or thing to another; a transferal.
An act of exiting one mass transit vehicle and boarding another (typically one belonging to a different line or mode of transportation) to continue a journey.
examples: Free transfers can be made between the Red and Orange Lines by walking from one of the station's platforms to another; in addition, riders using a fare card also get free transfers between bus and subway at the station.
